methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Evolv Technology operates in the Defense & Security sector as a public company listed on NASDAQ. The comparable universe comprises 2 public peers and 8 private companies, with notable private competitors including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. The company has raised over $400M to date.

Peer-median valuation methodology was not applicable in this analysis. The Defense & Security sector contains 21 same-sector peers overall, but the filtered peer set—applying constraints for stage alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, and valuation-tier similarity—yielded fewer than the required three datapoints necessary for robust median analysis. Constructing a median from an insufficient narrow peer set would introduce material distortion. Public comparable analysis using DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS would require additional financial metrics not provided in this dataset to generate defensible multiples.

The scarcity of direct comparables reflects the heterogeneity within Defense & Security subsectors and the limited number of public-market exits in this domain. Without sufficient narrow-peer triangulation or detailed financial data from public comparables, a quantitatively supported valuation range cannot be responsibly derived. Key uncertainties include Evolv's margin profile relative to defense-tech peers, growth trajectory versus public-market peers, and whether private comparables (Anduril, Shield AI) command venture-stage premiums or reflect maturer operational profiles. Further financial disclosure or expanded methodology would be required to support a specific valuation conclusion.
